source_pr.title: test: optimize session&planner/core unit test
source_pr.body:
What problem does this PR solve?
make several package unit test support run unit test parallel to speed up unit test running

What is changed and how it works?
optimize session&planner/core unit test and fix parallel run test problem.
- split session `testSessionSuite` into 2 test suites
- enable session & planner/core  unit test run in parallel by move cases depend on changing global variables into  serialSuite 
- fix several case that change global variables directly while it should be wrap by lock

optimize result:
```
>  go test -v -vet=off -p 10 -race github.com/pingcap/tidb/planner/core
ok  	github.com/pingcap/tidb/planner/core	116.440s

> go test -v -vet=off -p 10 -race github.com/pingcap/tidb/session
ok  	github.com/pingcap/tidb/session	93.397s
```

After change:
```
> go test -v -vet=off -p 10 -race github.com/pingcap/tidb/planner/core -check.p=true
ok  	github.com/pingcap/tidb/planner/core	31.602s

> go test -v -vet=off -p 10 -race github.com/pingcap/tidb/session -check.p=true
ok  	github.com/pingcap/tidb/session	65.009s
```

Check List
Tests

unit tests
Code changes

Side effects

Related changes

failure_signature: DATA RACE detected under `-race` when running planner/core + session tests in parallel (e.g. global `storeBootstrapped` map or shared suite state accessed concurrently).
root_cause_explanation: The flakiness is due to a real data race exposed by parallel test execution: running planner/core and session suites concurrently (e.g. `go test -p 10 -race ...`) can lead to concurrent access to shared global state (like the `storeBootstrapped` map) or shared suite data. The PR stabilizes it by adding proper locking for global state and marking specific suites/tests as serial-only (`SerialSuites`) so unsafe shared state isn't accessed concurrently.
fix_pattern: Make parallel tests race-safe: guard global maps/state with locks, and mark suites/tests as serial-only (or isolate per-test state) when they cannot safely run concurrently.

evidence: files: ddl/db_test.go, planner/core/physical_plan_test.go, planner/core/prepare_test.go, session/bootstrap_test.go, session/isolation_test.go, session/session.go, session/session_fail_test.go, session/session_test.go, session/tidb.go, session/tidb_test.go
